stem: A circle has radius $8$ cm and a sector with central angle $\dfrac{\pi}{4}$ radians. Find the arc length of the sector, to 2 decimal places.
hint: Use the arc length formula $l = r\theta$, with $\theta$ in radians.

stem: A sector has radius $6$ cm and central angle $\dfrac{2\pi}{3}$ radians. Find the area of the sector, to 2 decimal places.
hint: Use the sector area formula $A = \dfrac{1}{2}r^2\theta$, with $\theta$ in radians.

stem: A circle of radius $12$ cm has a sector with arc length $9\pi$ cm. Find the central angle of the sector in radians, to 2 decimal places.
A sector has radius $10$ m and central angle $\dfrac{3\pi}{5}$ radians. Find its arc length, to 2 decimal places.
A sector has radius $5$ cm and central angle $\dfrac{\pi}{3}$ radians. Find the area of the minor segment (the region between the chord and the arc), to 2 decimal places.
hint: Segment area = sector area $-$ triangle area. Use $A_{\triangle} = \dfrac{1}{2}r^2\sin\theta$.
explanation:
Sector area: $A_{\text{sector}} = \dfrac{1}{2}r^2\theta = \dfrac{1}{2}(25)\dfrac{\pi}{3} = \dfrac{25\pi}{6} \approx 13.09 \text{ cm}^2$

Triangle area: $A_{\triangle} = \dfrac{1}{2}r^2\sin\theta = \dfrac{1}{2}(25)\sin\dfrac{\pi}{3} = \dfrac{25}{2} \cdot \dfrac{\sqrt{3}}{2} = \dfrac{25\sqrt{3}}{4} \approx 10.83 \text{ cm}^2$

Segment area: $13.09 - 10.83 \approx 2.26 \text{ cm}^2$

stem: A circle has radius $9$ cm. A chord subtends a central angle of $\dfrac{2\pi}{3}$ radians. Find the area of the minor segment, to 2 decimal places.
hint: Segment area = sector area $-$ triangle area. Use $A_{\triangle} = \dfrac{1}{2}r^2\sin\theta$.
explanation:
Sector area: $A_{\text{sector}} = \dfrac{1}{2}(81)\dfrac{2\pi}{3} = 27\pi \approx 84.82 \text{ cm}^2$

Triangle area: $A_{\triangle} = \dfrac{1}{2}(81)\sin\dfrac{2\pi}{3} = \dfrac{81}{2} \cdot \dfrac{\sqrt{3}}{2} = \dfrac{81\sqrt{3}}{4} \approx 35.07 \text{ cm}^2$

Segment area: $84.82 - 35.07 \approx 49.75 \text{ cm}^2$

stem: A sector has radius $12$ cm and arc length $8\pi$ cm. Find the area of the sector, to 2 decimal places.
hint: First find $\theta$ using $\theta = l/r$, then use $A = \dfrac{1}{2}r^2\theta$.

stem: A sector has radius $6$ cm and area $15\pi$ cm². Find the arc length of the sector, to 2 decimal places.
hint: First find $\theta$ using $A = \dfrac{1}{2}r^2\theta$, then use $l = r\theta$.
explanation:
Find $\theta$: $15\pi = \dfrac{1}{2}(36)\theta = 18\theta$, so $\theta = \dfrac{15\pi}{18} = \dfrac{5\pi}{6}$ radians

Arc length: $l = r\theta = 6 \times \dfrac{5\pi}{6} = 5\pi \approx 15.71 \text{ cm}$
